summary: Webhook system full test — 49/49 backend tests passed, all frontend features verified. Fixed a critical bug in orders.py: 'payload.product_name' AttributeError (field doesn't exist in ManualOrderCreate model) that caused 500 on manual order creation, preventing dispatch_event from firing.

minor.issue: customer_email may be empty string if the customer record doesn't have an email field (email may be stored only in the users collection, not customers). Minor data mapping issue — customer_email appears as '' in webhook payloads.

action_items: FIXED (by T1): payload.product_name AttributeError in /app/backend/routes/admin/orders.py line 177 — changed to product.get('name', payload.product_id). This was causing 500 on manual order creation and preventing order.created dispatch.
LOW: Consider looking up customer email from users collection in dispatch_event so customer_email field in webhook payload is not empty for manually-created orders.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED: /app/backend/routes/admin/orders.py — ManualOrderCreate model does not have 'product_name' field but code accessed payload.product_name causing AttributeError → 500 on POST /api/admin/orders/manual. Fixed to use product.get('name', payload.product_id).
The field picker in the create webhook modal auto-expands when an event is subscribed (correct behavior). Expand chevron button toggles expanded state on/off — this is by design.
Delivery log detail shows full payload including event, tenant_id, timestamp, data (field-filtered) — working correctly.
HMAC signature: test delivery response from httpbin.org confirms X-Webhook-Signature header with sha256= prefix is present.
Tenant isolation is properly enforced — cross-tenant access returns 404 not 403 (leaks less info — this is acceptable).
seed_data_creation: Registered TEST_webhook_customer@example.com as a test customer in automate-accounts tenant for dispatch_event tests. Created and deleted test webhooks during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Webhook system is fully implemented and tested. Backend: 12 events across 5 categories (Orders/Subscriptions/Customers/Payments/Quote Requests), CRUD + test + rotate-secret + deliveries all working. HMAC signing with sha256= prefix verified. Tenant isolation working. dispatch_event fires on order.created (fixed bug in orders.py). Field filtering confirmed. Frontend: Webhooks tab in sidebar below API, webhook cards, create modal with event builder and field picker (auto-expands on subscribe), toggle Active/Pause, test delivery toast, delivery logs with detail view — all working. Test credentials: admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123! (partner_code: automate-accounts), adminb@tenantb.local / ChangeMe123! (partner_code: tenant-b-test).
rca_of_the_issue: BUG: POST /api/admin/orders/manual returned 500. Root cause: ManualOrderCreate Pydantic model defines product_id (not product_name), but the webhook dispatch call on line 177 of orders.py accessed payload.product_name which does not exist on the model instance — raising AttributeError that bubbled up as 500. Fix: replaced payload.product_name with product.get('name', payload.product_id) using the already-fetched product document. Reproduction: POST /api/admin/orders/manual with any valid payload would crash.
